meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio (Advanced)
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. An advanced-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' trap, build a SaaS tool featuring a robust Stripe-integrated pricing engine. By demonstrating complex logic like tiered subscription models, usage-based billing, and secure webhook handling, you move beyond simple CRUD apps to prove you can ship production-ready, revenue-generating software that impresses top-tier hiring managers.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of junior developer careers. You have the skills, but the lack of 'real-world' experience leaves you invisible to recruiters. The solution isn't another to-do list; it's the cold, hard reality of SaaS commerce. When you build a pricing engine, you aren't just coding a button—you are engineering the pulse of a business.

By integrating professional-grade subscription logic, you demonstrate mastery over state management, security, and third-party API orchestration. This is the difference between a student project and a technical asset. Welcome to the turning point where you stop being a learner and start becoming a builder.

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Define the data structure for tiered pricing (Free, Pro, Enterprise) ensuring scalability for future feature toggles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a multi-tier SaaS pricing model. Include tables for plans, subscriptions, and usage_limits with support for Stripe price IDs and recurring intervals.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate Prisma schema for SaaS subscription tables'

steps.title: Integrating Stripe Checkout
steps.description: Implement the backend logic to create Stripe sessions, moving beyond client-side hacks to secure server-side transaction handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure server-side function to handle Stripe Checkout sessions, mapping user IDs to customer metadata for seamless reconciliation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add stripe-node and scaffold checkout route

steps.title: Webhook Event Orchestration
steps.description: Build a robust webhook handler to sync Stripe subscription states with your local database, ensuring reliable billing lifecycle management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a production-ready Express webhook endpoint that listens for subscription.updated and invoice.payment_succeeded events to sync user access levels.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement webhook validation and event switch-case logic'

steps.title: Feature Gating with Middleware
steps.description: Create a middleware layer that checks subscription status against feature requirements, preventing unauthorized access to premium routes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable middleware function that validates user subscription status against a 'required_tier' attribute before allowing route access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate middleware to gate routes based on database subscription tier'

steps.title: Dashboard Usage Analytics
steps.description: Expose subscription metrics in a dashboard to provide users with transparency—a key feature in high-converting SaaS apps.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a dashboard component that fetches current subscription status and usage metrics from the backend to display visual progress bars for tier limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create API endpoint for subscription metrics and dashboard UI component'

pro_tips: Always implement idempotency keys in your Stripe API calls to prevent duplicate charges during network retries.
Use 'Stripe CLI' for local webhook testing instead of tunneling tools to avoid latency and security headaches.
Never hardcode pricing IDs; use environment-specific .env variables to manage development vs production environments.
Implement a 'grace period' logic in your database to handle failed payments without immediately locking the user out.
Build a 'Customer Portal' link into your UI so users can manage their own billing, significantly reducing support overhead.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to a platform like Vercel or Railway, configure your Stripe environment variables, and document the architecture diagram in your GitHub README to showcase your system design skills.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn a 'no portfolio' problem into a 'SaaS engineer' reality. Stop waiting for permission to build. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
